title: Aureline semantic-workspace-graph public node-identity boundary schema
description: Boundary schema for the public node-identity surface of the shared semantic-workspace-graph. This schema publishes one stable node-identity vocabulary every non-owning surface reads — search, docs, AI context assembly, review packs, support bundles, the public graph query family, and cited-explainer overlays — so no consumer mints a private node class, a private identity token, a private durable-versus-derived classification, or a private freshness / confidence vocabulary. The canonical internal shapes (graph_node_record, node_class, node_body, provenance_stamp, freshness_frame, confidence_level, confidence_rollup, source_anchor, workset_scope_ref, impact_reason_slot, explainer_citation_slot) are owned by schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json; this file is the public contract surface that references those canonical types and adds the publish-level rules: durable-versus-derived source-class partitioning, an authoritative-anchor requirement for durable identity, a published freshness-confidence rule aligned with the truth-class vocabulary in schemas/search/search_result_truth.schema.json, and a published audit-event vocabulary for node-identity lifecycle. Raw source bodies, raw symbol definitions, raw document bodies, and raw provider URLs never cross this boundary. Adding a new published node-identity-audit-event id, a new durable source-class entry, a new derived source-class entry, a new freshness-confidence compatibility pair, or a new published-identity-constraint id is additive-minor and bumps node_identity_schema_version. Repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

node_identity_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this node-identity export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

node_class.description: Frozen node-class vocabulary. Public identity reuses the canonical list owned by workspace_graph_seed.schema.json. file_node, directory_node, symbol_node, doc_node, ownership_node, topology_node, provider_resource_node, generated_artifact_node cover the canonical identity families spelled out in the M00-149 spec. imported_root_node, workset_scope_node, policy_view_node, and missing_anchor_node are also published so scope narrowing, imported content, policy projection, and unresolved references remain honest on the public surface rather than silently folded into local roots.

durable_source_class.description: Public subset of source_class that produces durable graph facts. A durable fact has a canonical producer the consumer can re-observe; public queries MAY render these rows without a derived-state disclosure. A node whose provenance_stamp.source_class is in this subset is a durable identity row.

derived_source_class.description: Public subset of source_class that produces derived graph facts. A derived fact is inferred, projected, or annotated; consumers MUST render a derived-state disclosure and MUST NOT upgrade the row to durable authority. A node whose provenance_stamp.source_class is in this subset is a derived identity row.

identity_durability_class.description: Published durability class. durable_identity: the node was emitted by a durable_source_class producer and MUST carry at least one source_anchor pointing at an authoritative anchor (filesystem_identity, symbol_definition_site, docs_pack_entry, mutation_journal_entry, generated_artifact_lineage, provider_resource_handle, imported_bundle_entry, replay_capture_entry, codeowners_rule). derived_identity: the node was emitted by a derived_source_class producer and surfaces the derived chip on every render. synthetic_identity: missing_anchor_node, policy_view_node, or workset_scope_node classes that stand in for honest absence or scope projection; synthetic identity MUST NOT be rendered as either durable or derived without its typed body badge.

freshness_confidence_compatibility_class.description: Published freshness-confidence compatibility class. Maps every legal combination of freshness_frame.freshness, confidence_level, and identity_durability_class onto a named public row-render posture. The posture names are the contract consumers read when deciding which truth-class badge to show. live_authoritative: freshness=authoritative, confidence=high, durable identity; renders the plain row. live_medium_confidence / live_low_confidence: freshness=authoritative, durable identity, confidence ≤ medium; renders a confidence chip. warming_partial: freshness=warming; renders the warming chip with the partial-truth disclosure contract from schemas/search/search_result_truth.schema.json. cached_authoritative_snapshot / stale_last_known_good / replayed_bundle_snapshot / imported_bundle_snapshot: non-live freshness frames; each MUST carry a stale_reason and render the matching chip. derived_inference: derived identity; renders the derived chip regardless of confidence. synthetic_honesty_row: synthetic identity; renders the missing-anchor, policy-hidden, or scope-projection badge.

published_identity_constraint_id.description: Published identity-contract constraint ids. Consumers, test packets, and support bundles cite these verbatim so a contract violation is named with one vocabulary rather than a surface-local synonym. node_id_stable_and_opaque: every record carries an opaque, stable node_id; retired ids are never reused. node_class_in_frozen_vocabulary: node_class matches the frozen list in workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#/$defs/node_class. durable_identity_requires_authoritative_anchor: a durable_identity row carries at least one source_anchor with an anchor_kind in the authoritative anchor set (filesystem_identity, symbol_definition_site, docs_pack_entry, mutation_journal_entry, generated_artifact_lineage, provider_resource_handle, imported_bundle_entry, replay_capture_entry, codeowners_rule). derived_identity_renders_derived_chip: a derived_identity row carries confidence_level ≤ medium unless the producer is an explicitly-authoritative inference owner (in which case the derived chip still renders). generated_artifact_identity_requires_lineage_ref: a generated_artifact_node carries a lineage_record_ref into schemas/workspace/generated_artifact_lineage.schema.json. missing_anchor_identity_requires_typed_reason: a missing_anchor_node carries a typed missing_reason. policy_view_identity_hides_member_list: a policy_view_node exposes only hidden_member_count. workset_scope_identity_first_class: a workset_scope_node is itself a graph node rather than a side-channel tag. freshness_confidence_class_matches_frame: the published freshness_confidence_compatibility_class matches the record's freshness_frame + confidence_level + identity_durability_class triple. explainer_citation_ref_reuses_graph_ids: explainer_citation_slot.citation_ref resolves to a graph node_id or edge_id (or a non-graph citation class). no_raw_body_crosses_boundary: raw source bytes, raw symbol definitions, raw document bodies, raw provider URLs, and raw credentials never appear in any field of a published node-identity record.

display_label.description: Redaction-aware display label surfaces render alongside the node id. Never substituted for node_id on links, citations, or deep-link anchors.

freshness_confidence_compatibility_class.description: Published compatibility class for this row. MUST align with the record's freshness_frame, confidence_level, and identity_durability_class; a mismatch is a contract violation (constraint id freshness_confidence_class_matches_frame).

scope_refs.description: One or more workset / scope refs the node is published under. A row invisible in the current scope carries visibility = missing_in_scope so consumers render an honesty badge rather than hiding the node.

source_anchors.description: Published source anchors. For identity_durability_class = durable_identity, at least one anchor MUST use an authoritative anchor_kind (filesystem_identity, symbol_definition_site, docs_pack_entry, mutation_journal_entry, generated_artifact_lineage, provider_resource_handle, imported_bundle_entry, replay_capture_entry, codeowners_rule). For derived_identity, anchors MAY be empty (the producer is the anchor, named in provenance_stamp). For synthetic_identity, anchors MUST be empty.

explainer_citations.description: Published explainer citations reusing graph node and edge ids. Overlays MUST NOT mint a private citation pointer.

published_notes.description: Redaction-aware reviewer notes carried on the published identity row. Never embeds raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.

node_identity_record.description: One published node-identity record. Mirrors schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#/$defs/graph_node_record with one additional rule: every published row carries an identity_durability_class and a freshness_confidence_compatibility_class so consumers render durable, derived, synthetic, cached, stale, replayed, imported, and warming states with one vocabulary rather than a local synonym.

node_identity_audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event id vocabulary for the published node-identity surface. Support bundles, replay captures, and AI evidence packets cite these verbatim. node_identity_constraint_violated MUST carry a violated_constraint_id.

notes_ref.description: Opaque ref into structured notes. MUST NOT embed raw source bodies, raw provider URLs, or raw credentials.
